rulings.Case Title: JESUS RODRIGUEZ MENDOZA VS. STEVE GORDON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 31, 2019, Line 2, PETITIONER JESUS MENDOZA's Petition For Writ Of Mandate. Petitioner Jesus Rodriguez Mendoza's petition for writ of mandate is denied. Mendoza fails to carry his burden to show that the administrative findings regarding his refusal to complete a chemical test are contrary to the weight of the evidence. The evidence shows that Mendoza agreed to submit to a breath test but failed to complete the test when, on each of three attempts, he refused to place his lips completely over the mouthpiece, causing an error. (See Cole v. Department of Motor Vehicles (1983) 139 Cal.App.3d 870, 875 [compliance with the implied consent law consists of completing, not merely attempting the test offered].) Mendoza's failure to complete the breath test constitutes a refusal. The evidence does not establish any officer-induced confusion regarding the refusal of the blood test. Mendoza agreed to a breath test again, after Officer Perdono read the Spanish version of the chemical admonition form. However, the officers already attempted in good faith to get Mendoza to complete the breath test. Completion of a blood test was the next available option. (See White v. Dept. of Motor Vehicles (2011) 196 Cal.App.4th 794, 799 ["When an arrestee choses one type of chemical test, the officer has wide discretion to determine whether that particular chemical test is available."]; Smith v. Dept. of Motor Vehicles (1986) 179 Cal.App.3d 368, 375.) There is no evidence that the officers acted in bad faith, but rather that they were trying to work with Mendoza by demonstrating and explaining the different tests. (People v. Sugarman (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 210, 216 ["Usually a test is not unavailable when an officer acts in bad faith by interfering with the defendants' choice of test."].) Petitioner, who did not testify at the hearing, provided no contrary evidence. (See Murphey v. Shimoto (2017) 13 Cal.App.5th 1052, 1070 [after department makes prima facie showing, burden shifts to driver to dispute the showing].) Thompson v. Department of Motor Vehicles (1980) 107 Cal.App.3d 354, relied on by Mendoza, is distinguishable because there, the driver's confusion arose from the inaudibility of the Section 13353 warning itself. Here, there is no evidence that Officer Perdono's admonishment was inadequate nor was there any apparent confusion akin to that of the driver in Thompson. (See Goodman v. Orr (1971) 19 Cal.App.3d 845, 857 [confusion occasioned by lack of intelligence or partial intoxication is immaterial].) Further, when Mendoza did repeatedly ask "what happens if I take it?", Officer Perdono clarified in Spanish. (See, e.g., Hoberman-Kelly v. Valverde (2013) 213 Cal.App.4th 626, 667 [officer obligated to attempt to clarify an arrested person's confusion over when the right to counsel arises].) Mendoza did not consent to take the test. Therefore, the evidence supports the ALJ's finding that Mendoza refused to submit to a blood test. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the respondent is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: DALE CHARLES VS. RICHARD MCCAMBRIDGE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 31, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANTS SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA TIMING ASSOCIATION, INC., BONNEVILLE NATIONALS, INC.'s MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File Cross-Complaint. Grant. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ENDOVENTION, INC. ET AL VS. CONFLUENT MEDICAL TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To (1) Plaintiff Endovention, Inc.S Request For Documents On Defendant Confluent Medical Technologies, Inc. - Set Two And (2) Plaintiff Endovention, Inc.S Request For Documents On Defendant Corpus Medical, Llc - Set Two; Memorandum In Support; Declaration Of Kirk B. Freeman; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 31, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFF ENDOVENTION, INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To (1) Plaintiff Endovention, Inc.'s Request For Documents On Defendant Confluent Medical Technologies, Inc. - Set Two And (2) Plaintiff Endovention, Inc.'s Request For Documents On Defendant Corpus Medical, LLC - Set Two. The court adopts the report and recommendation of the judge pro tem. Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to its requests for production of documents is granted. Defendants shall produce all responsive documents by January 3, 2020. Both parties' requests for sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
